pattern: Convert nums into a comparison array of -1/0/1 and use KMP substring matching to count occurrences of the pattern in O(n) time.
hints: Build an array cmp of length n-1 where cmp[i] = 1 if nums[i+1]>nums[i], 0 if equal, -1 if less; a matching subarray starting at index i requires cmp[i..i+m-1] == pattern.
This reduces to counting occurrences of pattern as a substring of cmp, which KMP solves in O(n+m) time.
Build the KMP failure function on pattern, then scan cmp character-by-character using the automaton to count full matches.

edge_inputs.description: strictly increasing, pattern [1,1] matches four subarrays
no match because equals breaks the pattern
pattern length equals nums length minus 1, one possible match
pattern of all -1 in a decreasing array
pattern of zeroes requires all equals

stress.description: large random arrays for KMP speed test
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    m = max(1, n // 10)
    pattern = [rng.choice([-1,0,1]) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums, pattern]
